full_text:
Organoid fixation and processing into FFPE blocks

Organoids cultured in 96-well plate with omentum gel coating are fixed and processed to FFPE blocks as follows:

Remove the culture medium, wash the wells with 150 ul / well 1x PBS

Add 150 ul of 10% formalin (1ml of formaldehyde + 9 ml 1X PBS) per well, and incubate at room temperature for 1 hr

Remove the formalin into a waste tube, wash the wells 2X with 150 uL1x PBS

Detach the matrix from the wells using a spatula and pool 5 wells into one Eppendorf and then to one block.

If extra liquid remains, quick spin the Eppendorf and remove the supernatant so that there is very little or no PBS left in the pellet.

Add 5 uL of Hematoxylin to the pellet followed by 130-150 uL of melted histogel* using pre-cut 200 uL tip (cut 200 uL tip to make it wide bore to avoid breaking of organoids while pipetting the histogel)

Mix well by pipetting 4-5 times, slowly drop the mixture into inside of the Eppendorf cap avoiding air bubbles

Allow the gel to solidify at +4C for 1 hr

Using the same thin scalpel used before, detach the solidified histogel from the Eppendorf cap and transfer it to pre-labelled histo cassette, fix the histogel plug over night at room temperature in 10 % formalin on a shaker (as done for tissues)

Next day, transfer the casettes to 70% EtoH, store at +4 up to one week or proceed with the processing into paraffin blocks. The program for tumor tissue processing and organoids is the same

*Melting of Histogel:

After fixing the wells, leave them in PBS until histogel is ready

Place the histogel tube in a glass container filled with tap water, and microwave at 800W, 30 sec x 3. Keep the tube in the same water until use
